codeReview.summary: The round-4 substring-arithmetic blocker is closed at current HEAD. Compared with the original AST rewrite in `f5523a2`, `walkInnerParameterOperation()` still routes both `Substring.offset` and `Substring.length` through `walkArithExpression()` (`packages/webapp/src/tools/bash-tool-allowlist.ts:462-466`), and `walkArithExpr()` now explicitly rejects both `ArithBracedExpansion` and `ArithSyntaxError` before the default-success branch (`packages/webapp/src/tools/bash-tool-allowlist.ts:514-595`). That makes the reported braced/syntax-error forms reject conservatively while preserving benign substring arithmetic and wildcard passthrough. The regression coverage at `packages/webapp/tests/tools/bash-tool-allowlist.test.ts:946-1114` now includes the pre-existing literal safe control (`${FOO:1:2}`), new braced length/offset and syntax-error rejection cases, a well-formed arithmetic safe control (`${FOO:$((1+1)):2}`), and wildcard `*` passthrough for all three bypass shapes. I did not find a remaining current-HEAD blocker that reproduces the round-4 substring-arithmetic bypass.

summary: Pass. Commit `2ba0c43` closes the residual `ArithBracedExpansion` / `ArithSyntaxError` substring-arithmetic bypass from round 4, and current HEAD still rejects the three reported forms while keeping benign substring arithmetic and wildcard passthrough intact. The added tests cover the new rejection cases plus the relevant safe-control and wildcard-preservation paths, and I did not find a new blocking problem in this fix scope.
